title: [lora_l2.h:70-71] LICHEN_LORA_FRAME_OVERHEAD includes signature but signature is conditional
description:
Category not checked: Protocol framing assumptions

Bug found: LICHEN_LORA_FRAME_OVERHEAD is defined as 55 bytes, which includes 48 bytes for Schnorr-48 signature. However, looking at the wire format:

From lichen_l2.c line 594-596:
  /* NULL dst_eui64 = broadcast (no destination address in frame header) */
  ret = lichen_link_tx(&link_ctx, tx_ipv6_buf, pkt_len, NULL,
                       tx_frame_buf, &frame_len);

The lichen_link_tx() function applies signature only if ctx->has_key is true. From link_ctx.h (external), has_key indicates whether the signing key is provisioned.

If no signing key is provisioned:
- Actual overhead = 5 (header) + 4 (MIC) = 9 bytes
- MTU could be 255 - 9 = 246 bytes, not 200

But LICHEN_LORA_MTU is defined as 200 regardless of signing key presence. This is conservative (safe) but wastes 46 bytes of payload capacity when no signing key is set.

The code always reserves space for signature even when not used.

Impact: P4 - 46 bytes of wasted MTU when signing is disabled (conservative, not a bug).
close_reason: Fixed: Added documentation explaining LICHEN_LORA_FRAME_OVERHEAD (55 bytes) is intentionally conservative, reserving space for signature even when not used. Static MTU simplifies buffer sizing.
